---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_daily_interaction.csv")

  # Variables
  date_var: "date"          # handles plain dates AND raw timestamps
  value_var: "adoption_rate" # ignored when aggregate = "count"

  # How to get one value per day:
  #   "none"  = data already has one row per day; use value_var directly
  #   "count" = count rows per day (e.g. one row per scan/visit/event)
  #   "mean" / "sum" = aggregate value_var per day
  aggregate: "none"

  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Daily Adoption Rate (%)"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "High-Frequency Tracker: Calendar Heatmap"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 3.5,
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers and values whitespace-scrubbed)
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)

# Validate columns: value_var is only required when it will actually be used
required <- params$date_var
if (params$aggregate %in% c("none", "mean", "sum")) {
  required <- c(required, params$value_var)
}
cerp_validate(raw_data, required)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
# 1. Parse dates robustly: "2025-11-01 04:36:41", ISO timestamps, plain dates
dated <- raw_data %>%
  drop_na(all_of(params$date_var)) %>%
  mutate(Date = cerp_parse_date(.data[[params$date_var]])) %>%
  drop_na(Date)

# 2. Collapse to exactly one value per day, per the aggregate parameter
daily <- switch(params$aggregate,
  "count" = dated %>% count(Date, name = "Value"),
  "mean"  = dated %>%
    mutate(V = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)) %>%
    group_by(Date) %>% summarize(Value = mean(V, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop"),
  "sum"   = dated %>%
    mutate(V = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]], params$value_var)) %>%
    group_by(Date) %>% summarize(Value = sum(V, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop"),
  "none"  = {
    if (anyDuplicated(dated$Date) > 0) stop(
      "Multiple rows share the same date, but aggregate is 'none'. ",
      "Set the 'aggregate' parameter to 'count' (rows per day), ",
      "'mean', or 'sum' so the template knows how to combine them."
    )
    dated %>% transmute(Date, Value = cerp_numeric(.data[[params$value_var]],
                                                   params$value_var))
  },
  stop("Unknown aggregate option: '", params$aggregate,
       "'. Use 'none', 'count', 'mean', or 'sum'.")
)

# 3. Missing Day Protection + calendar grid math
plot_data <- daily %>%
  complete(Date = seq.Date(min(Date), max(Date), by = "day")) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = year(Date),
    Month = month(Date, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE),
    DayOfWeek = wday(Date, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE, week_start = 1),
    first_day_of_month = floor_date(Date, "month"),
    first_day_wday = wday(first_day_of_month, week_start = 1),
    WeekOfMonth = ceiling((day(Date) + first_day_wday - 1) / 7),
    DayOfWeek = fct_rev(DayOfWeek)
  )

n_years <- n_distinct(plot_data$Year)
```


```{r plot-heatmap, fig.height=max(3.5, 3.2 * n_distinct(plot_data$Year))}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = WeekOfMonth, y = DayOfWeek, fill = Value)) +

  geom_tile(color = cerp_cols[["bg"]], linewidth = 0.6) +

  coord_equal() +

  # Sequential ramp: box tint (low) -> primary (high); missing days stay faint
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = unname(cerp_cols["box"]),
    high = unname(cerp_cols["primary"]),
    na.value = "#f1ede4",
    name = params$metric_name
  ) +

  theme_cerp() +

  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "left",
    legend.key.width = unit(1.8, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.title = element_text(family = cerp_font_caption, size = 10,
                                color = cerp_cols[["subtle"]]),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.25, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("High-Frequency Engagement:", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else ("One square per day. Darker means higher; pale squares are days with no data."),
    caption = params$source_note
  )

# Single year: months in one row. Multiple years: one row of months per year.
if (n_years > 1) {
  p + facet_grid(Year ~ Month)
} else {
  p + facet_wrap(~ Month, nrow = 1)
}
```
